Part B.key_evidence: First detection (ndethist=1, deltajd=0) with positive subtraction at r~20.09. Nearest PS1 source at 3.75 arcsec has sgscore1=0.018 (galaxy-like) with PS1 colors (g~20.25, r~19.92, i~19.46) consistent with a galaxy. classtar=0.98 and fwhm=1.13 pix indicate a point-like candidate. Science shows a point source; reference shows a nearby extended/resolved source slightly offset from the candidate; difference panel is noisy but no obvious dipole or edge artifact.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Supernova: a new point-like transient appearing offset from a PS1 galaxy neighbor (~3.8 arcsec), with no prior detection history at this position, positive subtraction, and reasonable SN-like magnitude. The host-like PS1 neighbor with galaxy sgscore and the single fresh detection support a young extragalactic transient.
Part B.alternative_analysis: Bogus/subtraction artifact is possible because the difference cutout appears predominantly noise-like without a clear compact positive residual, and sigmapsf=0.21 indicates a low-SNR detection. An asteroid is less likely given the coincidence with a PS1 galaxy and the lack of a streak. AGN is disfavored because sgscore1 indicates a galaxy and the offset (~3.8") suggests an off-nuclear location.
